title: Redundant 'ToString()' in string concatenation should be removed
messageFormat: Remove this redundant 'ToString()' call; string concatenation already converts the operand.
summary: When an expression takes part in a string concatenation, the compiler already calls ToString() on each non-string operand. An explicit ToString() in that position is pure noise and, with a null operand, can even turn a safe empty result into a NullReferenceException.
